articles.title: Zambia cancels world’s largest human rights and tech summit days before start
articles.description:
Government blocks RightsCon 2026 conference saying it did not ‘align with national values’
The world’s largest conference on human rights and technology has been cancelled just days before it was due to start after the Zambian government told organisers it did not align with “national values”.
Zambia’s government had originally welcomed the RightsCon 2026 summit on “human rights in the digital age”, due to be held in the capital, Lusaka, on 5-8 May, but Thabo Kawana, permanent secretary for the Ministry of Information & Media, said last week that the conference would not go ahead to allow time to ensure the gathering “aligns with Zambia’s national values, policy priorities, and broader public interest considerations”.
 Continue reading...

articles.title: First malaria drug for babies is approved in ‘major public health milestone’
articles.description:
WHO prequalification of Coartem Baby means newborns can be safely treated rather than using medication for older children
The first malaria treatment for babies has been approved by the World Health Organization, opening the door to widespread use around the globe.
In parts of Africa, up to 18% of children under six months will be infected with malaria, but there has historically been no safe treatment for the smallest of them. There were 610,000 deaths from malaria in 2024, about three quarters of which were under-fives in Africa.
 Continue reading...
articles.titleJa: 赤ちゃん向け初のマラリア薬が承認、「公衆衛生の画期的な出来事」に

articles.title: Sabastian Sawe receives hero’s welcome in Kenya after sub-two hour marathon feat
articles.description:
Record-breaker says London Marathon win was ‘a victory for all of us’ as he is greeted by family and friends in Eldoret
Hugged, cheered and adorned with garlands, the first man to run an official marathon in under two hours has returned as a hero to his home village in Kenya.
Sabastian Sawe, who stunned the world when he clocked 1h 59m 30s in the London Marathon last weekend, flew in a Kenyan military plane normally reserved for special operations on Thursday to his home region of western Kenya.

articles.title: Uganda copying Russia and China with new bill designed to crush dissent, say critics
articles.description:
New law proposes up to 20 years in prison for promoting ‘foreign interests’, and restricts those who work with or are funded by overseas partners
Ugandan opposition figures, human rights organisations and legal experts have condemned a sweeping bill that proposes up to 20 years in prison for promoting “foreign interests”, and imposes restrictions on a broad range of people and organisations that work with or receive funding from overseas partners.
The protection of sovereignty bill 2026 is being fast tracked through parliament, with debate expected to conclude before the presidential swearing-in on 12 May.
 Continue reading...

articles.title: BAE faces £120m lawsuit over decision to scrap support for aid aircraft
articles.description:
EnComm Aviation says the firm’s action has cut off vital support for crisis-hit countries including South Sudan and the DRC
Britain’s biggest weapons manufacturer, BAE Systems, is facing a £120m lawsuit after scrapping support for aircraft used to deliver aid to some of the world’s neediest countries.
EnComm Aviation, a Kenya-based aid cargo operator, claims the decision forced the cancellation of humanitarian contracts and reduced supplies to South Sudan, now threatened by famine, Somalia and the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC), among others.

articles.title: German museum to return rare Irritator dinosaur skull to Brazil
articles.description:

It is a 113-million-year-old bone of contention.
After Stuttgart’s museum of natural history bought a fossilised dinosaur skull in 1991, researchers found it was the most complete spinosaurid skull known to date, belonging to a previously unknown genus of the huge meat-eating dinosaurs.

articles.title: Cuba says Trump’s fresh sanctions on its economy amount to ‘collective punishment’
articles.description:
The US sanctions target people operating in broad sections of Cuban economy, including energy, defence and mining

Cuba’s government has said new sanctions imposed on the island by Donald Trump amounted to “collective punishment”, as an enormous 1 May procession outside the American embassy in Havana vowed to “defend the homeland”.
In an executive order on Friday, the US president said he would impose sanctions on people involved in broad sections of the Cuban economy, as he seeks to put more pressure on Havana after ousting Venezuela’s leader, Nicolás Maduro, earlier this year.

articles.title: Pope appoints former undocumented immigrant as bishop of West Virginia
articles.description:
Leo, who has criticized Trump’s hardline immigration policy, selected Evelio Menjivar-Ayala as state’s new bishop

Sign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inbox

Pope Leo XIV has appointed a man who had once entered the United States as an undocumented immigrant, hidden in the trunk of a car, as the new bishop of West Virginia.
The pope approved the resignation of Bishop Mark E Brennan of Wheeling-Charleston, West Virginia, and selected Bishop Evelio Menjivar-Ayala, 55, of Washington to take his place, reported OSV News.
 Continue reading...

articles.title: Could Santa Marta climate talks mark ground zero in push to ditch fossil fuels?
articles.description:
Colombia hosted nearly 60 countries at pivotal time on world stage for fight to transition to a clean energy future
Looking out to sea from the grey sandy beaches of Santa Marta, on Colombia’s Caribbean coast, it is never hard to spot evidence of the country’s thriving fossil fuel export trade. Oil tankers ride at anchor on the horizon and sometimes, locals say, lumps of coal wash up on the shore, blown off the collier ships that carry cargos from the nearby mines.
It was here, on Wednesday evening, that the Colombian government took a bold step to shift its economy – and that of the rest of the world – away from dependence on coal, gas and oil and into a new era of clean energy. With the first ever conference on “transitioning away from fossil fuels”, the host joined nearly 60 countries determined to loosen of the grip of petrostates on the world’s future.
 Continue reading...

articles.title: Antigua and Barbuda prime minister set to win fourth term in election
articles.description:
Gaston Browne is on course to win 15 of the 17 seats in parliament after calling snap election
Gaston Browne, the prime minister of Antigua and Barbuda, is set to win a fourth term in the country’s snap general election with preliminary results showing his party on course to win 15 of the 17 seats in parliament.
Addressing supporters early on Friday morning, Browne said: “You have spoken, you have spoken clearly. You have indicated that the Antigua and Barbuda Labour party (ABLP) is the best institution to run this country.”

articles.summary: 世界アンチ・ドーピング機関（WADA）が、2019年のモスクワ研究所データ押収後にロシア人選手300件以上のドーピング違反を認定したと発表した。

articles.title: Trainee driver crashes bus into River Seine
articles.description: The driver hit a parked car and veered off the road into the river - about 12 miles south of Paris - early on Thursday.
articles.titleJa: 見習い運転手、バスをセーヌ川に衝突させる
articles.summary: 見習い運転手が、パリの南約12マイルでバスを運転中に駐車車両に衝突し、道を逸れてセーヌ川に転落する事故を起こした。

articles.title: Israel intercepts Gaza flotilla near Crete and detains 175 activists
articles.description: Pro-Palestinian activists say 22 boats carrying aid for Gaza were illegally intercepted in international waters near the Greek island.
articles.titleJa: イスラエル、クレタ島沖でガザ支援船団を阻止し活動家175人を拘束
articles.summary: イスラエル軍が、ギリシャのクレタ島付近の国際水域でガザへ向かう支援船団を阻止し、船に乗っていた親パレスチナ活動家175人を拘束した。

articles.title: Hungary's next PM says frozen EU funds will be paid out soon
articles.description: Péter Magyar meets EU leaders in Brussels, for the first time since his Tisza party won a landslide election.
articles.titleJa: ハンガリー次期首相、凍結されたEU資金は近く支払われると発言
articles.summary: ハンガリーの次期首相は、ブリュッセルでEU首脳と会談後、凍結されていたEU資金が間もなく支払われると発表。国内政治に大きな影響を与える。

articles.title: Russia scales back Moscow Victory Day parade, blaming threat from Ukraine
articles.description: Russia says the annual commemoration of Soviet victory in World War Two will not feature military vehicles or cadets.

articles.title: US charges Mexican governor and other leaders with aiding drug cartel
articles.description: The governor of Sinaloa, Rubén Rocha Moya, hails from the same party as the president of Mexico.
articles.titleJa: 米国、メキシコ知事らが麻薬カルテルを支援したとして起訴
articles.summary: 米国がシナロア州知事を含むメキシコの指導者らを麻薬カルテル支援の容疑で起訴した。知事はメキシコ大統領と同じ政党に属している。

articles.title: Global forest loss slows but El Niño fires could threaten progress
articles.description: The loss of tropical rainforests eased last year, according to new analysis, but scientists warn they are still disappearing rapidly.
articles.titleJa: 世界の森林減少は減速するも、エルニーニョによる火災が進行を脅かす可能性
articles.summary: 新しい分析によると、昨年の熱帯雨林の喪失は緩和されたが、科学者たちは依然として急速に消滅していると警告する。エルニーニョによる火災がこの進捗を脅かす可能性も指摘されている。

articles.title: US soldier accused of betting on Maduro's removal pleads not guilty to fraud charges
articles.description: The Army officer has been accused of using classified information to win $400,000 on an online prediction market.
articles.titleJa: 米兵、マドゥロ大統領罷免への賭けで詐欺罪の無罪を主張
articles.summary: 米兵が機密情報を用いてオンライン予測市場で40万ドルを獲得した詐欺罪に対し、無罪を主張している。マドゥロ大統領の罷免を巡る賭けが焦点となっている。

articles.title: Indian billionaire's son offers to save Escobar's hippos
articles.description: Anant Ambani - son of Asia's richest man Mukesh Ambani - says he can take care of the animals roaming free since the drug lord's death.
articles.titleJa: インドの億万長者の息子、エスコバルのカバを救うと申し出る
articles.summary: アジア一の大富豪ムケシュ・アンバニ氏の息子アナント・アンバニ氏が、麻薬王エスコバルの死後、野放しになっているカバたちの世話を申し出た。

articles.title: Mexican cartel leader found hiding in a ditch
articles.description: Audias Flores Silva was a key player in the Jalisco New Generation Cartel, whose top leader was killed two months ago.
articles.titleJa: メキシコのカルテルリーダーが用水路に隠れているところを発見される
articles.summary: ハリスコ新世代カルテルの主要人物であるアウディアス・フローレス・シルバが、用水路に隠れているところを発見された。同カルテルの最高リーダーは2か月前に殺害されたばかりだった。

articles.title: Will Mexico City's airport be ready for the World Cup?
articles.description: The BBC takes a closer look at major renovations at Benito Juárez International Airport aimed at boosting capacity ahead of the 2026 World Cup.
articles.titleJa: メキシコシティの空港はワールドカップに間に合うのか？
articles.summary: 2026年ワールドカップに向けて、ベニート・フアレス国際空港での大規模な改修工事が容量増強を目指している。準備状況についてBBCが詳しく調査する。

articles.title: The other life of US soldier accused of betting on Maduro's removal
articles.description: The Army officer charged with using classified information to win $400,000 is also a real estate investor with rave reviews on Airbnb.
articles.titleJa: マドゥロ大統領罷免への賭けで告発された米兵のもう一つの顔
articles.summary: 機密情報を用いて40万ドルを獲得した容疑で起訴された米陸軍将校が、Airbnbで高評価を得る不動産投資家としての別の顔を持つことが明らかにされた。

articles.title: Making history and facing Neymar - Lingard on life in Brazil
articles.description: In his first major interview since moving to Brazil, Jesse Lingard talks to the BBC about his new life, and Manchester United.
articles.titleJa: 歴史を刻みネイマールと対峙 – リンガード、ブラジルでの生活を語る
articles.summary: ブラジル移籍後初の主要インタビューで、ジェシー・リンガードがBBCに対し、ブラジルでの新しい生活とマンチェスター・ユナイテッドについて語った。

articles.title: Voice notes are massive in some countries but not the UK - here's why
articles.description: Voice notes have they taken some countries by storm, while failing to truly take off in Britain.
articles.titleJa: ボイスノートは一部の国で大流行しているが英国ではそうではない – その理由とは
articles.summary: ボイスノートが一部の国で大流行している一方で、英国では普及していない理由が分析されている。これはコミュニケーションツールの利用習慣における文化的な違いを示唆している。

articles.title: 'We cried together': Trump's deportation drive forces tough decisions for couples
articles.description: The spouses of illegal immigrants facing deportation must choose between separation or moving to a new country.
articles.titleJa: 「共に泣いた」：トランプの強制送還政策が夫婦に厳しい決断を迫る
articles.summary: トランプ政権下の強制送還政策により、不法移民の配偶者たちは、離れ離れになるか、新しい国へ移住するかの厳しい選択を迫られている。

articles.title: The human cost of the data center push in Latin America
articles.description: In this cross-border article, Global Voices is sharing stories from several countries in Latin America where communities are pushing back against AI-driven data center development.
articles.titleJa: ラテンアメリカでのデータセンター推進の人道的コスト
articles.summary: ラテンアメリカの複数の国で、AI駆動型データセンター開発に地域社会が反発し、その人道的コストが浮き彫りになっている現状を報じる。

articles.title: Brazil: A warning on how AI and deepfakes can become an ’excessive risk‘ to women and girls
articles.description: A technical note by research center Internetlab points out that advances in synthetic image creators increase the risks of gender violence online and talks about platforms’ accountability
articles.titleJa: ブラジル：AIとディープフェイクがいかに女性や少女にとって「過剰なリスク」となるかという警告
articles.summary: ブラジルで、AIとディープフェイク技術の進歩が女性や少女に対するオンラインでのジェンダー暴力のリスクを高め、プラットフォームの責任を問う警告が発せられている。

articles.title: AI hype narrative reaches the public healthcare system in El Salvador
articles.description: This time we take you to El Salvador, examining government claims that an AI-supported transformation of the public healthcare system will make it the most advanced in the world.
articles.titleJa: エルサルバドルの公衆衛生システムにAIの誇大宣伝が到達
articles.summary: エルサルバドルで、政府がAIを活用した公衆衛生システムの変革により世界最先端になると主張。その実態と課題が注目されている。

articles.title: The technological transformation of Colombia's armed conflict
articles.description: More than simply a uniform adoption of artificial intelligence, a technological innovation has emerged that is transforming the battlefield, decision-making processes, local narratives, and forms of social control.
articles.titleJa: コロンビアの武力紛争における技術的変革
articles.summary: コロンビアの武力紛争が技術革新により変容。AI以上の技術が戦場、意思決定、地域の物語、社会統制の形を変化させている。

articles.title: When dry season never ends
articles.description: In an era marked by intense transnational mobility, the uneven distribution of rights and experiences in host communities reflects underlying dynamics of power and privilege that demand further scrutiny.
articles.titleJa: 乾季が終わらないとき
articles.summary: 国境を越える人の移動が激しい時代において、受け入れコミュニティにおける権利と経験の不均衡が、根底にある権力と特権の力学を反映している。

articles.title: Community micro-hydropower plants revolutionise isolated areas of the Dominican Republic
articles.description: Community micro-hydropower projects in the DR rely on local leadership to deliver energy from renewable sources to isolated areas excluded from national distribution systems, contributing to reduced greenhouse gas emissions.
articles.titleJa: ドミニカ共和国の孤立地域を改革するコミュニティ小水力発電所
articles.summary: ドミニカ共和国の孤立地域で、地域主導の小水力発電プロジェクトが再生可能エネルギーを供給し、温室効果ガス排出削減に貢献している。

articles.title: How Bad Bunny brought the issue of Puerto Rico’s power grid into world view
articles.description: Puerto Rico’s electricity system has faced repeated crises since 2017’s Hurricane Maria, which destroyed much of the grid and triggered the longest blackout in modern U.S. history; outages remain routine.
articles.titleJa: バッド・バニーがプエルトリコの電力網問題を世界に知らしめた方法
articles.summary: バッド・バニーが、ハリケーン・マリア以来、度重なる危機に直面し、停電が日常化しているプエルトリコの電力網問題を世界に提起した。

articles.title: Ecuador’s Netanyahu and Trump supporters target Ecuadorian–Iranian Cultural Center
articles.description: Wars no longer have defined frontlines. Battlefields can be anywhere those in power decide that a neighbor, due to their faith, ethnicity, or background, is an enemy.
articles.titleJa: エクアドルのネタニヤフとトランプ支持者がエクアドル・イラン文化センターを標的に
articles.summary: エクアドルで、ネタニヤフおよびトランプ支持者がエクアドル・イラン文化センターを標的に。これは、信仰や民族性を理由に隣人を敵と見なす権力者の動きとして報じられている。

articles.title: Concerns grow as Jamaica ends Cuban medical programme
articles.description: Terminating the decades-old programme that brought hundreds of Cuban medical personnel to its shores is an unusual move, especially for a programme that had been running smoothly for 50 years.
articles.titleJa: ジャマイカ、キューバの医療プログラム終了で懸念高まる
articles.summary: ジャマイカが50年間続いていたキューバ人医療従事者派遣プログラムを終了し、医療への影響と外交関係に関する懸念が高まっている。

articles.title: From menstrual dignity to digital safety: How grassroots feminists are redefining gender justice
articles.description: What connects a pad box in a Nigerian school, a coalition meeting in Pakistan, and a digital security workshop in Paraguay? They each address a structural barrier that limits women’s participation.
articles.titleJa: 生理の尊厳からデジタル安全まで：草の根フェミニストがいかにジェンダー公正を再定義するか
articles.summary: 生理の尊厳、デジタル安全、そして女性の参加を制限する構造的障壁に取り組む草の根フェミニストたちが、ジェンダー公正を世界的に再定義している。

articles.title: The world wants to eliminate cervical cancer - how Australian scientists led the way
articles.description: For the first time, Australia has recorded no new cervical cancer diagnoses in women under 25. To get there, they vaccinated young men.
articles.titleJa: 世界が子宮頸がんの撲滅を目指す中、オーストラリアの科学者はいかに道を切り開いたか
articles.summary: オーストラリアで25歳未満の女性における子宮頸がんの新規診断が初めてゼロに。若い男性へのワクチン接種が成功の鍵となった。世界的な病気撲滅に向けた科学的進展を示す。

articles.title: Myanmar ex-leader Aung San Suu Kyi moved to house arrest, military says
articles.description: The Nobel Peace Prize laureate has been in detention since she was ousted in a military coup in 2021.
articles.titleJa: ミャンマーのアウン・サン・スー・チー元指導者、自宅軟禁に移送と軍が発表
articles.summary: 2021年の軍事クーデターで失脚し拘束されていたノーベル平和賞受賞者アウン・サン・スー・チー氏が、軍の発表により自宅軟禁に移送された。ミャンマーの政治状況に影響を与える動き。

articles.title: It's not just oil: Iran war also threatens Asia's food security
articles.description: Planting season is beginning in Asia - but the closure of the Strait of Hormuz and Chinese restrictions has hit fertiliser supply.
articles.titleJa: 石油だけではない：イラン戦争はアジアの食料安全保障も脅かす
articles.summary: アジアで植え付けシーズンが始まる中、ホルムズ海峡の閉鎖と中国の規制が肥料供給に打撃を与えている。イラン戦争は石油だけでなく、食料安全保障にも広範囲な経済的影響を及ぼしている。

articles.title: Violence in Australian town after arrest of man over girl's murder
articles.description: Unrest took place outside hospital where man suspected of murdering a five-year-old girl was being treated.
articles.titleJa: 少女殺害容疑の男逮捕後、オーストラリアの町で暴力事件発生
articles.summary: 5歳の少女を殺害した容疑で男が逮捕された後、治療を受けていた病院の外で騒乱が発生した。オーストラリアの町で発生した深刻な犯罪とその後の社会的な混乱に関するニュース。

articles.title: A 17th Century 'supercomputer' once owned by Indian royalty sells for record in auction
articles.description: The astrolabe - or astronomical computer - is possibly the largest in existence and had never been exhibited before.
articles.titleJa: 17世紀の「スーパーコンピューター」、かつてインド王族が所有、オークションで記録的な価格で落札
articles.summary: 17世紀にインド王族が所有していたとされる天体観測儀（アストロラーベ）が、オークションで記録的な価格で落札された。現存するものとしては最大級で、これまで未展示だった貴重な科学的遺物。

articles.title: China to ban drone sales in Beijing citing security concerns
articles.description: Drone users will also be required to register their devices and secure permits before flying them.
articles.titleJa: 中国、安全保障上の懸念から北京でのドローン販売を禁止へ
articles.summary: 中国は安全保障上の懸念を理由に、北京でのドローンの販売を禁止する。ドローン使用者には、デバイスの登録と飛行許可の取得も義務付けられる。政府による技術規制の動き。

articles.title: Singapore court fines women for pro-Palestinian walk
articles.description: This reverses an earlier acquittal of the activists, who had led a walk to support the Palestinian cause.
articles.titleJa: シンガポール裁判所、親パレスチナ行進を行った女性たちに罰金
articles.summary: シンガポール裁判所は、パレスチナ支持の行進を主導した活動家たちの無罪判決を覆し、罰金を科した。言論の自由や政治的表現に関する国内の議論を反映する判決。

articles.title: Christchurch mass killer loses bid to overturn conviction
articles.description: White supremacist Brenton Tarrant's appeal is "utterly devoid of merit", a New Zealand court has ruled.
articles.titleJa: クライストチャーチの大量殺人犯、有罪判決覆す申し立てを棄却される
articles.summary: ニュージーランドの裁判所は、クライストチャーチで大量殺人を行った白人至上主義者ブレントン・タラントの控訴を「全く根拠がない」として棄却した。事件の司法手続きが終結に向かう。

articles.title: Jewish agency warned of 'high' threat level ahead of Bondi shooting, report finds
articles.description: Gun reforms should be prioritised and policing around Jewish events increased, royal commission says.
articles.titleJa: ボンダイ銃乱射事件前、ユダヤ系機関が「高い脅威レベル」を警告していたと報告書
articles.summary: ボンダイ銃乱射事件に先立ち、ユダヤ系機関が高い脅威レベルを警告していたことが報告書で判明。銃規制の優先とユダヤ関連行事での警備強化が提言された。

articles.title: Kim Jong Un praises troops who 'self-blasted' to avoid capture by Ukraine
articles.description: It confirms suspicions that North Korean soldiers are being told to detonate their grenades to kill themselves.
articles.titleJa: 金正恩、ウクライナに捕獲されるのを避けるため「自爆」した兵士を称賛
articles.summary: 北朝鮮の金正恩総書記が、ウクライナによる捕獲を避けるために「自爆」した兵士たちを称賛した。これにより、北朝鮮兵士が捕虜になることを避けるために手榴弾で自決するよう指示されている疑惑が確認された。

articles.title: WTO opens online registration for 2026 Public Forum, launches call for proposals
articles.description: The WTO has opened online registration for this year’s Public Forum, titled Powering the Future, which will take place at the WTO premises in Geneva from 15 to 17 September. It has also launched a call for proposals for the event, which this year will focus on services trade.
articles.titleJa: WTO、2026年公開フォーラムのオンライン登録を開始し、提案を募集
articles.summary: WTOは、9月にジュネーブで開催される2026年公開フォーラムのオンライン登録と提案募集を開始した。今回はサービス貿易に焦点を当てる。

articles.title: Canada commits CAD 500,000 to strengthen food safety and plant and animal health capacity
articles.description: Canada is providing CAD 500,000 (approximately CHF 286,000) to the Standards and Trade Development Facility (STDF) to strengthen sanitary and phytosanitary (SPS) capacity of public and private sector stakeholders in developing and least-developed countries. The contribution will support STDF activities that help countries meet international food safety and plant and animal health standards and facilitate safe trade, based on the STDF Strategy for 2025-2030.
articles.titleJa: カナダ、食品安全および動植物衛生能力強化のため50万カナダドルを拠出
articles.summary: カナダは、開発途上国における食品安全および動植物衛生能力強化のため、STDFに50万カナダドルを拠出することを表明した。

articles.title: Members review notifications, anti-dumping actions at regular spring meeting of Committee
articles.description: The WTO’s Committee on Anti-Dumping Practices met on 28 April 2026 to review members’ latest notifications of new, amended or previously reviewed anti-dumping laws and regulations as well as reports on anti-dumping actions.
articles.titleJa: 委員会定期会合で加盟国が通知、アンチダンピング措置を検討
articles.summary: WTOのアンチダンピング措置委員会が定期会合を開催し、加盟国による新しいアンチダンピング法規の通知と、措置に関する報告を検討した。

articles.title: WTO members review safeguard actions, exchange views on measures affecting steel products
articles.description: At a meeting on 27 April, the WTO Committee on Safeguards reviewed safeguard actions covering 38 products, 12 of which were steel/metal products. These actions were notified by members since the Committee's last meeting in October 2025. Several members expressed their views on the European Union's and the United Kingdom's actions concerning steel products. The EU investigation on grain-oriented electrical steel and measure on ferro-alloying elements also generated interventions by several members. The outgoing Chair, Ms Milagros Miranda Rojas (Peru), thanked members for their participation in the Committee.

articles.title: WTO project aims to step up ePing use to increase transparency and market access in Africa
articles.description: Implementation of a WTO initiative to strengthen use of the ePing SPS & TBT Platform is now under way, helping governments, exporters and other stakeholders better track and engage in evolving product requirements affecting international trade. Funded by the Standards and Trade Development Facility (STDF), the three year project focuses on five African countries and aims to enhance transparency, predictability and market access.
articles.titleJa: WTOプロジェクト、アフリカにおけるePing活用を強化し透明性と市場アクセス向上へ
articles.summary: WTOは、アフリカ5カ国を対象にePing SPS & TBTプラットフォームの活用を強化し、国際貿易における透明性と市場アクセスを向上させるプロジェクトを開始した。

articles.title: TRIPS Council considers technology transfer, digitalization and non-violation complaints
articles.description: At a meeting of the Council for Trade-Related Aspects of Intellectual Property Rights (TRIPS) on 23-24 April, items discussed by WTO members included technology transfer, digitalization of IP offices, and TRIPS non-violation and situation complaints. In the meeting, chaired by Emmanuelle Ivanov-Durand of France, members were also updated on notifications under various provisions of the TRIPS Agreement, including through the WTO Secretariat's sixth Annual Transparency Report. Mr Alaa Hegazy of Egypt was elected chair for the coming year.
articles.titleJa: TRIPS理事会、技術移転、デジタル化、非侵害苦情を審議
articles.summary: TRIPS理事会で、技術移転、知的財産庁のデジタル化、TRIPS協定の非侵害苦情などがWTO加盟国間で議論された。

articles.title: India launches safeguard investigation on soda ash
articles.description: On 24 April 2026, India notified the WTO’s Committee on Safeguards that it had initiated on 16 March 2026 a safeguard investigation on imports of soda ash.
articles.titleJa: インドがソーダ灰に対するセーフガード調査を開始
articles.summary: インドは、2026年3月16日にソーダ灰の輸入に対するセーフガード調査を開始したことをWTOセーフガード委員会に通知した。

articles.title: DG Okonjo-Iweala welcomes Chief Executive Sam Hou Fai of Macao, China to the WTO
articles.description: Director-General Ngozi Okonjo-Iweala met with the Chief Executive of Macao, China, Sam Hou Fai on 23 April at the WTO. They discussed the active role Macao, China plays at the WTO and its strong support for the multilateral trading system. As trade openness and transparency are key to Macao, China's strong economic performance, both leaders highlighted the importance of the WTO for supporting the ongoing contribution of Macao, China to the global economy.
articles.titleJa: オコンジョ＝イウェアラ事務局長、マカオ（中国）の賀一誠行政長官をWTOに歓迎
articles.summary: オコンジョ＝イウェアラ事務局長は、マカオ（中国）の賀一誠行政長官と会談し、マカオのWTOでの役割と多角的貿易システムへの強力な支持を議論した。

articles.title: Canada launches safeguard investigation on certain wood goods
articles.description: On 23 April 2026, Canada notified the WTO’s Committee on Safeguards that it had initiated on 21 April 2026 a safeguard investigation on imports of certain wood goods.
articles.titleJa: カナダが特定木材製品に対するセーフガード調査を開始
articles.summary: カナダは、2026年4月21日に特定木材製品の輸入に対するセーフガード調査を開始したことをWTOセーフガード委員会に通知した。

articles.title: Forty-six women-led businesses in Jordan to benefit from WEIDE digital trade programme
articles.description: Forty-six women-led businesses in Jordan have been selected as beneficiaries of the Women Exporters in the Digital Economy (WEIDE) Fund, allowing them to begin a year-long programme to strengthen their participation in international trade. The announcement was made in Amman on 23 April at a launch event hosted by the Jordan Enterprise Development Corporation (JEDCO), WEIDE's partner business support organization in the country.
articles.titleJa: ヨルダンの女性主導ビジネス46社がWEIDEデジタル貿易プログラムから恩恵を受ける
articles.summary: ヨルダンの女性主導ビジネス46社がWEIDE基金の受益者に選ばれ、デジタル貿易プログラムを通じて国際貿易への参加を強化する。
